surface.name: Create an Okta Integrator Free Plan org and complete an Angular SPA redirect sign-in
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create an Okta Integrator Free Plan org and complete an Angular SPA redirect sign-in. Okta recommends the hosted redirect model as the most secure and fastest sign-in integration, and its official Angular SPA guide ends with a signed-in profile view. Prior surface before policy commit: Unresolved at application type and language selection in Okta Quickstart.

alternatives_considered.name: Server-side web app redirect quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Requires a framework choice among ASP.NET Core, Node.js, Java, Go, Python, and others.

alternatives_considered.name: SPA redirect quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A peer front-end type with public-client and PKCE configuration.

alternatives_considered.name: Mobile/native redirect quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: A peer type with device-specific SDK steps.

alternatives_considered.name: Protect your API
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Verifies API access rather than an end-user sign-in.

entry_point.developer_intent: Obtain an Okta Integrator Free Plan org and choose the app/runtime whose redirect-authentication or API-protection journey should be measured.
assumed_prior_state: The developer does not yet have an Okta Integrator Free Plan org.
No application type, language, or authentication target is supplied.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The Okta Angular SPA quickstart ends when the Profile view displays the signed-in user's name and the Sign out control is available.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The Profile view displays the user's name and the signed-in controls include Sign out.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Profile view displays the user's name and the signed-in controls include Sign out.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The Profile view displays the user's name and the signed-in controls include Sign out.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: A free Okta Integrator Free Plan org with an administrator account, created with a unique eligible business email. The org does not expire, but deactivates after 180 consecutive days with no user sign-in unless an app is submitted to the OIN, and it permits 10 active users.
A declared application type, runtime/framework, and intended outcome.

primary_path.action: Open the Okta Quickstart.
details: The page organizes rapid integration paths by application type.
primary_path.success_signal: Sign users in and Protect your API are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Open Okta Sign up and select the Okta Workforce Identity tile's Sign up for Integrator Free Plan route.
details: The current signup surface distinguishes Auth0, a 30-day Okta Platform trial, and the free Okta Integrator Free Plan.
The Integrator Free Plan is the developer route for building, testing, and managing workforce integrations.
primary_path.input: Intent to build and test an Okta integration.
primary_path.success_signal: First Name, Last Name, Work Email, and Country/Region fields are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting the 30-day Okta Platform trial or Auth0 creates a different account and product path.

primary_path.action: Enter first name, last name, location, and a unique business email address.
details: The live form labels the email Work Email and location Country/Region.
The same business email cannot create multiple Integrator Free Plan organizations.
Some email addresses can be rejected based on location and security considerations.
primary_path.output: Completed Integrator Free Plan identity fields.
primary_path.success_signal: All required fields pass form validation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A reused, ineligible, or security-restricted business email blocks org creation.

primary_path.action: Review the Integrator Free Plan Agreement and Privacy Policy, confirm authority to bind the organization, choose the optional marketing consent, and submit Sign up.
details: The signup states that clicking Sign up binds the developer or represented entity to the Integrator Free Plan Agreement and Privacy Policy.
Okta's current subscription agreement says a person accepting on behalf of a customer must have authority and may not use a free trial if they do not agree.
primary_path.input: Completed form and authority to accept the applicable terms.
primary_path.success_signal: Okta accepts the submission and sends the verification email.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Lack of authority or refusal of required terms blocks signup; marketing consent is optional.

primary_path.action: Send the Integrator Free Plan verification email.
details: The email includes the new Okta org domain and an account-activation link.
primary_path.output: Verification email with org domain and activation link.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer receives the message and can identify the org domain.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email delivery or business-email eligibility can delay or block activation; no delivery time is documented.

primary_path.action: Open the activation link and set the administrator password and other authenticators.
details: The verification email is the documented transition to password and authenticator setup.
primary_path.success_signal: Password and required authenticators are configured and the org can be opened.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Expired, missing, or already-used activation state requires account recovery, which the signup reference does not decompose.

primary_path.action: Open the new Okta org's Admin Console and retain its org domain.
details: The Integrator Free Plan includes SSO, Universal Directory, adaptive MFA, Lifecycle Management, API Access Management, and Workflows, subject to its documented limits.
The org is free and does not expire, but 180 days without any user sign-in deactivates it unless an app has been submitted to the OIN.
primary_path.input: Activated administrator account and org domain.
primary_path.output: Usable Integrator Free Plan org with administrator access.
primary_path.success_signal: The Admin Console opens and the org domain is known.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The plan supports no more than 10 active users, and an inactive org can deactivate after 180 days.

primary_path.action: Choose sign-in or API protection, then the application type.
details: Sign-in types are server-side web, front-end web, and mobile/native.
API protection is separate.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide family matches the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The roster entry supplies no app architecture.

primary_path.action: Choose the documented language or framework implementation.
details: Each app family offers its own runtime variants.
primary_path.success_signal: A guide with concrete integration and verification steps is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Research stops rather than imposing a runtime.

primary_path.action: Open Okta Sign up and choose Sign up for Integrator Free Plan from the Workforce Identity option.
details: The signup page also offers Auth0 and a 30-day Okta Platform trial, which are different routes.
primary_path.input: Intent to build and test a Workforce Identity integration.
primary_path.output: The Integrator Free Plan registration form.
primary_path.success_signal: The name, work email, and country or region fields are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Selecting another plan or Auth0 changes the organization and product path.

primary_path.action: Enter first name, last name, country or region, and a unique eligible business email.
details: The same business email cannot create multiple Integrator Free Plan organizations.
primary_path.success_signal: All required identity fields pass validation.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A reused or restricted email can be rejected based on location or security considerations.

primary_path.action: Accept the required Integrator Free Plan agreement and privacy terms, confirm authority to bind the organization, choose optional marketing consent, and submit Sign up.
details: Marketing consent is optional, but acceptance of the applicable agreement and privacy policy is required.
primary_path.input: The completed form and authority to accept the terms.
primary_path.success_signal: Okta accepts the submission and initiates email verification.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Lack of authority or refusal of the required terms blocks signup.

primary_path.action: Send the organization verification email with the org domain and activation link.
details: This message is the documented handoff from registration to administrator setup.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer receives the org domain and activation link.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Email delivery is an explicit wait and no delivery time is documented.

primary_path.action: Open the activation link and configure the administrator password and required authenticators.
details: The activation flow establishes the first org administrator.
primary_path.input: The verification email and activation link.
primary_path.success_signal: The password and authenticators are accepted.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing, expired, or used link requires recovery that the signup reference does not decompose.

primary_path.action: Sign in to the new org's Admin Console and retain the org domain.
details: The Integrator Free Plan does not expire, but an org can deactivate after 180 days with no user sign-in and is limited to 10 active users.
primary_path.input: The activated administrator and org domain.
primary_path.output: Administrator access to the Integrator Free Plan org.
primary_path.success_signal: The Admin Console opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication failure or a deactivated org blocks application setup.

primary_path.action: Open Applications, Applications, select Create App Integration, choose OIDC and Single-Page Application, then select Next.
details: OIDC SPA is the app-integration type used by the Angular redirect guide.
primary_path.success_signal: The form shows SPA-specific grant and URI settings.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing another protocol or application type produces a different integration.

primary_path.action: Name the integration, enable Authorization Code and Refresh Token, and configure the local sign-in and sign-out URIs.
details: Set the sign-in redirect URI to http://localhost:4200/login/callback and the sign-out redirect URI to http://localhost:4200.
primary_path.input: An app name and the documented local URIs.
primary_path.success_signal: Both grant types and exact callback URIs are present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A mismatched redirect URI prevents Okta from returning authentication to the sample.

primary_path.action: Choose the controlled-access assignment, save the integration, then use the General tab to verify Refresh Token, set the Initiate login URI, save again, and copy the Client ID.
details: The selected user must be allowed to access the application.
On the General tab, confirm Refresh Token is selected and set the Initiate login URI to the local application URI documented by the guide before selecting Save.
primary_path.input: The completed SPA settings and intended test-user assignment.
primary_path.output: A saved OIDC SPA integration and client ID.
primary_path.success_signal: The integration exists, Refresh Token and Initiate login URI match the guide, the settings are saved, and the client ID is copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unassigned test user, missing Refresh Token grant, or incorrect Initiate login URI prevents the documented sign-in path from working as configured.

primary_path.action: Add http://localhost:4200 as an Okta Trusted Origin for the required local redirect and CORS behavior.
details: The guide explicitly enables the local base URI in Trusted Origins.
primary_path.output: A trusted local origin in the Okta org.
primary_path.success_signal: http://localhost:4200 appears in Trusted Origins with the required types.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing trusted origin can block browser requests or redirect behavior.

primary_path.action: Confirm a recent Node and npm environment, then create the Angular 22 app with npx @angular/cli@22 new okta-angular-sample --ssr=false.
details: The guide disables server-side rendering for this browser SPA sample.
primary_path.input: Node, npm, and a writable project directory.
primary_path.success_signal: The okta-angular-sample directory is created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported runtime, package download, or scaffolding errors block setup.

primary_path.action: Enter the project and install @okta/okta-angular@8.0 and @okta/okta-auth-js@8.0.
details: These documented package versions provide the Angular integration and browser authentication client.
primary_path.output: The Okta Angular and Auth JS dependencies.
primary_path.success_signal: npm completes installation without error.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dependency-resolution errors prevent configuration.

primary_path.action: Ensure the default custom authorization server has an access policy, then configure the Angular application with that issuer, the copied Client ID, redirect URI, scopes, and Okta authentication provider.
details: The default custom authorization server must have an access policy before it can authorize this application.
The redirect URI must resolve to http://localhost:4200/login/callback and the issuer must use the new org domain's default authorization server.
primary_path.input: The Okta org domain, default authorization-server access policy, client ID, and guide's Angular configuration.
primary_path.output: An Angular app connected to the OIDC SPA integration.
primary_path.success_signal: The authentication provider and OktaAuth configuration contain no placeholders.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing authorization-server access policy or wrong issuer, client ID, callback, or scopes causes authorization or callback failure.

primary_path.action: Add the sign-in and sign-out controls, login callback route, and the guide's initial unguarded Profile route and view.
details: The callback component processes Okta's redirect, while the Profile route provides the visible authenticated result.
Do not label this initial Profile route as protected: the guide adds canActivateAuthGuard later, after the initial sign-in and profile test.
primary_path.output: A runnable SPA with authentication UI and routes.
primary_path.success_signal: The app includes Sign in, callback, the initial unguarded Profile route and view, and Sign out behavior.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing callback or route configuration leaves the redirect incomplete.

primary_path.action: Run ng serve and open http://localhost:4200.
details: The Angular development server hosts the configured SPA on the registered origin.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser loads the SPA and shows the Sign in control.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build or port errors prevent the browser flow.

primary_path.action: Select Sign in, authenticate as an assigned org user, and allow the callback to return to the SPA.
details: Okta hosts the credential experience and returns the browser to the registered callback.
primary_path.input: Credentials for an assigned Okta user.
primary_path.success_signal: The callback completes and the SPA changes to its signed-in state.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid credentials, an unassigned user, mismatched URI, issuer, or client ID blocks the redirect flow.

primary_path.action: Open Profile and verify that the signed-in user's name is displayed, then confirm Sign out is available.
details: The profile content is the guide's observable proof that the SPA obtained and used the authenticated user state.
primary_path.output: A visible user profile in the Angular app.
primary_path.success_signal: The Profile view displays the user's name and the signed-in controls include Sign out.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A completed login without the profile result does not satisfy this candidate terminal.

branches.path: Use the recommended Okta-hosted redirect model, then choose server-side, SPA, or mobile and a framework.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success as completing hosted sign-in and observing authenticated user state or claims.

branches.path: Choose Protect your API and a server framework, configure authorization, send a token-bearing request, and verify allowed versus denied responses.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Defines success around API authorization rather than interactive sign-in.

branches.condition: The app is a single-page application.
branches.path: Create an OIDC SPA integration, configure Authorization Code with PKCE and redirect URIs, enable trusted origins, install the framework SDK, implement sign-in/out and protected routes, then sign in.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds public-client, CORS, PKCE, and browser SDK actions.

branches.condition: The app is server-side, mobile, or native.
branches.path: Follow the matching app type, redirect URI, credential, SDK, callback, and verification sequence.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes credential handling and callback implementation by runtime.

friction_gates.description: An Okta Integrator Free Plan org and administrator access are required; signup needs a unique eligible business email and a location accepted by Okta's security controls.
Signup requires acceptance of the Integrator Free Plan Agreement and Privacy Policy by someone authorized to bind the represented entity.
The developer must receive the verification email and use its activation link before setting the password and authenticators.
The free org is limited to 10 active users and deactivates after 180 consecutive days without a user sign-in unless an app is submitted to the OIN.
The administrator needs sufficient role permissions to create and configure app integrations.
Existing orgs without API Access Management may require support enablement or a Console-only alternative.
The developer must choose sign-in versus API protection and an application architecture.
A language or framework choice is required before atomic implementation steps exist.

sources.title: Sign users in overview
evidence_supported: Hosted redirect as recommended, most secure, fastest model

sources.title: Sign users in to your SPA using the redirect model
Basics: Create an app integration
sections_used: How to create an app integration
evidence_supported: Integrator Free Plan and admin requirements

sources.title: Okta Integrator Free Plan org configurations
sections_used: Sign up for an Integrator Free Plan org
evidence_supported: Workforce Identity plan selection, name/location/business-email form, verification email, org domain, activation link, password and authenticators, no-expiry rule, 180-day inactivity deactivation, 10-user limit, and unique-email rule

sources.title: Okta Developer sign up
evidence_supported: Auth0, 30-day Okta Platform trial, and Integrator Free Plan distinction; current required fields; required agreement and privacy acceptance; optional marketing consent; inactivity notice

sources.title: Okta Master Subscription Agreement
evidence_supported: Authority-to-bind and acceptance gate for free plans and trial services

uncertainties.question: Which application type and runtime should be measured?
uncertainties.impact: It determines integration configuration, code, and terminal success.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Quickstart explicitly requires these choices and the roster gives only the company.

uncertainties.question: Which password and authenticator screens appear after the activation link?
uncertainties.impact: Authenticator policy and recovery requirements can add device, factor-enrollment, and verification steps before the Admin Console opens.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current org-defaults reference establishes the activation link, password, and other authenticators but does not enumerate their exact in-product screens or factor choices.

excluded_after_success: No implementation-specific downstream work can be excluded until app type, runtime, and first-success boundary are chosen.
